title: rootless: call IsRootless just once
body:
we are calling this function several times, it is worth to store its
result and re-use it.

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
/approve

Nice catch!

Do you think there are similar candidates in the code? Hunting those down could be a nice entry-level task to get familiar with different parts of the code. But for that, we need to give some examples to begin with.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> Do you think there are similar candidates in the code? Hunting those down could be a nice entry-level task to get familiar with different parts of the code. But for that, we need to give some examples to begin with.

from a quick look in the rootless code, I didn't spot any easy candidate, but probably there are :-)

I'd like to reduce the additional cost of rootless containers.  What I really hate is the additional process we are using, but I don't know how that could be solved without requiring a third process to setup the user namespace.

I've tried to at least reduce the number of threads of the first since all it does is to wait for the re-execed one, but I didn't manage to find anything in Go that could help with it.  `GOMAXPROCS` doesn't seem to influence the number of threads spawned by the Go Runtime

LGTM
Love improvements like this, especially when I get to learn some new go functionality.
